paper_title: Computational methods and data visualization for diatom analysis

methods.description: This method involves preprocessing and augmenting images of wild and modified diatoms to create a balanced training dataset for neural network training. The process ensures equal representation of both diatom types and applies transformations to simulate variations in SEM images.

steps.description: Select 29 images of wild diatoms and 29 images of modified ones.
steps.searchable_summary: select equal number of wild and modified diatom images

steps.description: Randomly select the type of image to add to the training set, then randomly draw a specific image of that type.
steps.searchable_summary: randomly select image type and specific image for training set

steps.description: Rescale all images using resize function from opencv to have the same pixel/nm value of 3.
steps.searchable_summary: rescale images to uniform pixel/nm value using opencv

steps.description: Randomly select 250 × 250 px cutaways from a randomly selected image.
steps.searchable_summary: select random 250x250 px cutaways from images

steps.description: Apply vertical and horizontal mirroring at random.
steps.searchable_summary: apply random vertical and horizontal mirroring

steps.description: Rotate the cutaway using opencv functions getRotationMatrix2D and warpAffine.
Apply non-linear adjustment of contrast for the training set to incorporate possible artifacts from SEM images.

methods.description: This method describes the architecture, training, and validation of a neural network (NN 1) designed to classify images of wild and modified diatoms. The network's performance is validated to ensure it differentiates based on morphology rather than noise.

steps.description: Create NN 1 architecture with two convolutional layers, a max pooling layer, and a dense layer using Keras with TensorFlow backend.
steps.searchable_summary: design NN 1 architecture with Keras and TensorFlow

steps.description: Use ReLUs as activation functions and a two-unit dense layer with softmax activation for classification.
steps.searchable_summary: implement ReLUs and softmax activation in NN 1

steps.description: Optimize the network with the Adam optimizer using the cross-entropy metric.
steps.searchable_summary: optimize NN 1 with Adam optimizer and cross-entropy

steps.description: Train NN on a randomly selected subset of images, with 80% as training set and 20% as validation set.
Validate NN performance by retraining with only wild diatom images split into two sets with different labels.
steps.searchable_summary: validate NN 1 by retraining with identical class images

methods.description: This method uses the CrossVis system to visualize and analyze multivariate data of diatom parameters, allowing for interactive exploration of class interrelationships and parameter correlations.

steps.description: Inspect class interrelationship between diatom parameters using CrossVis.
steps.searchable_summary: use CrossVis to visualize diatom parameter relationships

steps.description: Use parallel coordinates plot to represent N-dimensional tuples with points on N parallel axes.
steps.searchable_summary: create parallel coordinates plot for multivariate data

steps.description: Select parameter ranges of interest by dragging a rectangular region on the vertical axes.
steps.searchable_summary: interactively select parameter ranges on plot

steps.description: Display mean and mean-centered standard deviation range within each vertical axis.
steps.searchable_summary: show mean and standard deviation on plot axes

steps.description: Represent categorical parameters to study wild vs. modified classes.
steps.searchable_summary: visualize categorical parameters for class study
